---
title: My dynamic report
output: html_fragment
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, error=FALSE)
library(tidyverse)
```

```{css}
body {
  font-family: Arial, sans-serif;}
h2 {
color: #2a9d8f;}
```

## Correlation between having breakfast and energy level / academic performance

```{r}
logged_data <- read_csv("https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/e/2PACX-1vQlLGalkDcTXRSb5mvHVcNRidq0cMtJmkd4MZus-iT66FtF_i_dpJkRaxSwLetdj_9CA1umeS3xUfAh/pub?output=csv")

#Renaming data frame labels
latest_data <- logged_data %>%
  rename(
    breakfast = "Did you have breakfast this morning?",
    last_eat = "What did you last eat before your first class today?",
    energy_rating = "How energized did you feel today? (0–10)",
    academic_rating = "How would you rate your academic performance today? (0–10)"
  )

# Compute and assign labels to statistics for dynamic comments
mean_academic <- mean(latest_data$academic_rating, na.rm = TRUE)
mean_energy <- mean(latest_data$energy_rating, na.rm = TRUE)

```

### Dynamic Summary

Based on the collected data:

- The **average academic performance** across all students is `r round(mean_academic, 2)` out of 10.
- The **average energy rating** is `r round(mean_energy, 2)` out of 10.

```{r}
# Bar chart showing last thing eaten before first class
ggplot(latest_data, aes(x = last_eat)) +
  geom_bar(fill = "lightblue") +
  labs(
    title = "Types of Last Foods Eaten Before First Class",
    x = "Last Thing Eaten",
    y = "Count"
  ) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 60, hjust = 1)) #Rotate the text for better visibility

# Box plot showing how you feel about your academic performance academic based on breakfast
ggplot(latest_data, aes(x = breakfast, y = academic_rating)) +
  geom_boxplot(fill = "lightgreen") +
  labs(
    title = "Academic Performance (0–10) by Breakfast",
    x = "Had Breakfast?",
    y = "Academic Rating"
  )
```
